scope: The two loop gates that scraped model PROSE — the design-TL;DR gate (required literal §1–§6 headers) and the reqverify Definition-of-Ready gate (scraped a `## RESULTS` prose section) — now consume schema-constrained structured JSON via the ADR-0011/ADR-0012 StructuredOutput transport, so a StructuredOutput-capable model that does not reproduce the exact prose shape (as Grok did not) passes; a model without the capability degrades to a declared Rule 2 deferral naming the missing capability, never a silent pass and never a hard prose-format fail.

test_results.output: ok — all 47 test packages pass, 0 failures (full suite: newline-eating-corruption + cross-package fixture-regression guard per memory; internal/design 0.03s, internal/reqverify 0.01s, internal/model 2.14s, internal/driver 2.10s, internal/driver/inprocess 0.17s, internal/implement 0.67s, internal/run 7.03s, cmd/sworn 29.7s)
ok — AC-04 combined command (spec §5 reachability): internal/design, internal/reqverify, internal/model all pass
ok — driver-contract seam + DoR integration + CLI: internal/driver (+inprocess/drivertest/registry), internal/implement, cmd/sworn all pass

clean — no gofmt diffs across the 20 changed .go files, no vet findings; newline-eating-corruption grep over changed .go returned no fused '//'+code (three grep hits were benign comment prose referencing String()/RunFirstPass())

reachability_artifacts: AC-01 design gate: internal/design/tldr_test.go:TestGenerateWritesSixSections + TestGenerateGrokCase drive design.Generate (the gate slice.go:335 dispatches on the captain driver) — TestGenerateGrokCase feeds a fake whose PROSE has NO §1–§6 headers but whose StructuredJSON is valid, and asserts Generate PASSES and writes a design.md carrying the deterministically-generated `## §1`..`## §6` headers (the exact Grok failure, now passing).

AC-01/AC-02 driver seam: internal/driver/inprocess/inprocess_test.go:TestInprocessCaptainStructured drives dispatchCaptain with StructuredSchema set — proves exactly ONE ChatStructured (response_format) request, no investigation loop, and Result.StructuredJSON returned unmodified; TestInprocessCaptainProseUnchanged proves the nil-schema prose path is untouched.

AC-02 DoR gate: internal/reqverify/reqverify_test.go:TestRun_StructuredNoProseResults drives reqverify.Run over a structured emission asserted to contain NO `## RESULTS` prose and PASSES (the Grok DoR failure, now passing); internal/implement/ready_test.go exercises CheckDoR -> reqverify.Run (ready.go:106) with structured passing/failing replies.

AC-03 design deferral: internal/design/tldr_test.go:TestGenerateCapabilityAbsentDeferral — a fake returning driver.ErrKindUnsupported yields errors.Is(err, design.ErrStructuredUnsupported) and NO design.md written; slice.go's existing generic genErr arm records it as a Rule 2 deferral (recordDesignGateDeferral, slice.go:343).

AC-03 DoR deferral: internal/implement/ready_test.go:TestCheckDoR_CapabilityAbsentDeferral drives CheckDoR with a capability-absent verifier and asserts DoR fails closed (ReqverifyPassed=false) through the 'not evaluated' arm with a capability-naming reason — never a hard error, never a silent pass; internal/reqverify TestRun_CapabilityAbsentDeferred proves Run surfaces report.Deferred + reason; internal/driver/inprocess TestInprocessCaptainStructuredRequiresStructuredOutput proves the driver returns ErrKindUnsupported at the seam.
delivered: AC-01: the design-TL;DR gate accepts a valid schema-constrained structured design object regardless of prose header formatting — internal/design/tldr.go (designEmitSchema + designTLDR typed parse + deterministic render, hasSixSections retired for the non-empty missingSections check); internal/design/tldr_test.go:TestGenerateGrokCase (prose lacks §1–§6, structured valid -> PASS) + TestGenerateWritesSixSections.

AC-02: the reqverify DoR gate parses a structured results object, not a `## RESULTS` prose scrape — internal/reqverify/reqverify.go (reqverifyResultsSchema + parseStructuredGrades + validateReqverifyResults lightweight validate, replacing parseGrades/acResultRe); internal/reqverify/reqverify_test.go:TestRun_StructuredNoProseResults + TestParseStructuredGrades_* .

AC-03: capability-absent is a DECLARED Rule 2 deferral distinct from a hard failure — D1 dispatchCaptain type-asserts model.StructuredOutput and returns new driver.ErrKindUnsupported (internal/driver/subprocess.go) when absent; the design gate maps it to design.ErrStructuredUnsupported (tldr.go) surfaced by slice.go's deferral arm, and the DoR gate routes it through CheckDoR's 'not evaluated' arm via reqverify.Report.Deferred (reqverify.go + ready.go). Tests: TestGenerateCapabilityAbsentDeferral, TestCheckDoR_CapabilityAbsentDeferral, TestRun_CapabilityAbsentDeferred, TestInprocessCaptainStructuredRequiresStructuredOutput.

AC-04: acceptance semantics preserved verbatim — design PASS = all six fields non-empty (missingSections reproduces hasSixSections); DoR PASS = identical per-AC fail-closed grade logic (AC missing -> FAIL, malformed emission -> BLOCK) on the parsed results array; prior-model stubs still pass (adapted, not rewritten, per D5). `go test ./internal/design/... ./internal/reqverify/... ./internal/model/...` green; full `go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./...` green (47 pkgs, 0 fail).

D1 (Type-1, ADR-0012 amendment, Coach-ratified): DispatchInput.VerdictSchema renamed StructuredSchema (role-agnostic) across every call site (driver.go, claude.go, codex.go, verify.go, inprocess*.go, drivertest/conformance.go, and tests); dispatchCaptain gains a structured path (one-shot ChatStructured, no loop) when the schema is set, prose Chat path unchanged when nil; Result.StructuredJSON returned uniformly. ADR-0012 amendment note appended (docs/adr/0012-driver-contract.md).

D3 (Type-1-adjacent, Coach-ratified): driver.ErrKindUnsupported = "unsupported" added to the binding cross-driver taxonomy (subprocess.go); NOT terminal (TerminalErrKind stays {auth,credits}); the constant lives in the contract package so subprocess-family drivers can map it too.

D2 (Coach-confirmed): sworn-local inline emit schemas (title-named, strict-subset — no minLength/pattern/format) — designEmitSchema (emit-only, an artefact) and reqverifyResultsSchema (emit + lightweight validateReqverifyResults, a fail-closed gate). No canonical Baton *-v1.json added; no fork under an existing $id.

Wire-boundary integrity (S06 AC-04 / TestNoWireImports): the CLI structured adapter routes through model.ChatStructuredJSON (new helper in internal/model, wire-type construction encapsulated there) rather than referencing model.ChatMessage in cmd/sworn — the orchestration path stays wire-type-free.
not_delivered: Model-backed `sworn verify` (agentic proof-bundle gate) and `sworn llm-check --check ac-satisfaction` were not run in this implementer session: no live provider key available. Deterministic evidence stands in — full `go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./...` green, gofmt/vet clean, newline-eating grep clean, and the AC-mapped tests above. The mandatory fresh-context /verify-slice pass (Rule 7) is the model-backed check for this slice; the verifier session owns it (no separate issue).

Interpreter/orchestrator prose scrapers off the design/DoR critical path were NOT swept — declared out_of_scope in spec.json. Per Coach flag §4, if that later sweep is real follow-on work it must be filed as an issue before it is worked; it is not owned by this slice.

Subprocess-family drivers (claude.go/codex.go) do not add a behavioural structured-captain path: they do not declare RoleCaptain (sworn#86) so the design/DoR gates never reach them; D3 only requires the ErrKind be mappable there, which the shared contract-package constant satisfies. No behavioural change to subprocess drivers beyond the compile-checked field rename.
divergence: internal/model/client.go was edited (add ChatStructuredJSON helper + model.ErrStructuredUnsupported sentinel) although the declared touchpoints list internal/model/ (the directory) generically. The helper exists to keep the `sworn reqverify` CLI adapter off the internal/model wire types (ChatMessage) that TestNoWireImports (S06 AC-04) bans in cmd/sworn — the wire construction is encapsulated inside internal/model, which the guard does not scan. Within the spec's internal/model/ touchpoint; surfaced for visibility.

cmd/sworn/reqverify.go was edited (structuredReqverifier adapter + deferral exit path). design.md §3 flagged this as wiring-only 'if the reqverify.Verifier interface signature changes' (Pin 4, MECHANICAL) — the signature did change (added the schema []byte arg and the structured return), so the CLI adapter update is the anticipated lockstep change, not new scope.

internal/run/slice.go was NOT edited (design §5 named slice.go:335 as the design-gate integration point). The existing generic genErr -> recordDesignGateDeferral arm already surfaces design.ErrStructuredUnsupported as a Rule 2 deferral, so no slice.go edit was needed — consistent with design §3 not listing slice.go under files-to-touch.
